title: Best Dividend ETFs in Canada 2026: XDV, VDY, CDZ Compared
description: Compare the best Canadian dividend ETFs in 2026 including XDV, VDY, CDZ, and ZDV. Analyze yields, MERs, and holdings for TSX dividend investors.

schemas.headline: Best Dividend ETFs in Canada 2026: XDV, VDY, CDZ Compared
schemas.description: Compare the best Canadian dividend ETFs in 2026 including XDV, VDY, CDZ, and ZDV. Analyze yields, MERs, and holdings for TSX dividend investors.

mainEntity.name: What is the best dividend ETF in Canada 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 'best' dividend ETF depends on your goals. VDY typically offers high yield through Canadian bank stocks, while CDZ focuses on dividend aristocrats and dividend growth. Compare MERs and top holdings to align with your strategy.

mainEntity.name: Which is better XDV vs VDY?

acceptedAnswer.text: VDY generally has a lower Management Expense Ratio (MER) and broader exposure, while XDV holds fewer stocks and has a higher MER. VDY is heavily weighted towards Canadian financials.

mainEntity.name: Are Canadian dividend ETFs tax-free in a TFSA?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, dividends paid by Canadian ETFs held within a Tax-Free Savings Account (TFSA) are completely tax-free. They are also tax-sheltered in an RRSP.

mainEntity.name: What is the highest yield ETF in Canada?

acceptedAnswer.text: High yield ETFs often change rank, but covered call ETFs (like ZWC) or highly concentrated dividend ETFs often provide the highest raw yield, though they may trail in total return compared to VDY or XDV.

mainEntity.name: Do Canadian dividend ETFs pay monthly?

acceptedAnswer.text: Many popular Canadian dividend ETFs, including VDY, XDV, and CDZ, pay distributions on a monthly basis, making them popular for income-seeking investors.
